data.title: CVS Health Stock Surges After Earnings Smash Forecasts. A Value Play Has Become a Rally. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Josh Nathan-Kazis and Mackenzie Tatananni

Shares of CVS Health jumped in premarket trading Thursday after the pharmacy and healthcare giant posted solid second-quarter earnings and boosted its full-year outlook.

Adjusted earnings of $1.81 a share handily topped the $1.46 analysts had forecast, according to FactSet. Revenue of $98.9 billion also came in above the $94.5 billion Wall Street was anticipating.

CVS Health raised its full-year guidance, saying it now expects adjusted earnings in the range of $6.30 to $6.40 a share, up from $6 to $6.20. Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting $6.12 a share. The company also boosted its cash flow from operations guidance to at least $7.5 billion from roughly $7 billion.

Shares climbed 8.6% to $67.66 in premarket trading. Healthcare peers traded mixed. UnitedHealth Group and Humana fell 1.1% and 0.5%, respectively, while Cigna rose 2.7%.

CVS Health operates through three segments, namely health care benefits, health services — which includes its pharmacy benefits manager — and pharmacy and consumer wellness.

The company's health care benefits segment posted a medical cost ratio of 89.9% in the quarter, slightly below the 90.5% analysts were expecting. The metric tracks the proportion of premiums paid out to cover medical expenses.

CVS Health's Medicare Advantage business has presented a challenge for the healthcare giant in recent years. But isn't alone. Peers like UnitedHealth have also been hard-hit by rising medical costs and changes to the Medicare Advantage framework.

Still, the owner of the sprawling CVS Pharmacy drug store chain and the insurance giant Aetna has been a rare good-news stock for healthcare investors this year. Shares remain up 39% in 2025, even as the S&P 500 Health Care sector index has fallen 2%.

That's a welcome reversal for a stock that has been a long-term underperformer. Shares fell 57% from the start of 2022 through the end of 2024 as CVS's retail pharmacy struggled with a troubled business model, its pharmacy benefit manager faced increasing government scrutiny, and its insurer stumbled as patients sought more medical care.

In late 2024, the company elevated a top executive, David Joyner, to replace Karen Lynch as CEO, and investors developed a newfound interest in the stock.

What may have started as a value play has evolved into a legitimate rally. The company reported fourth-quarter earnings that beat estimates in February, and beat estimates again when it reported first-quarter earnings in May.

CVS Health's stock price is still nearly 40% short of where it was trading at the end of 2021. But the latest earnings report has led to renewed enthusiasm, as evidenced by the market reaction Thursday. Now it's time to see if the company can keep its hot streak going.

Write to Josh Nathan-Kazis at josh.nathan-kazis@barrons.com and Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Chile’s Industrial Growth Slows in June
data.description:
Industrial production in Chile rose 3.2% year-on-year in June 2025, easing from the 4.6% increase in May and marking the weakest performance since February.

Growth was driven by a 12% surge in manufacturing output, up from 2.9%, largely due to a 17.8% rise in food production, followed by paper products (+14.2%) and beverages (+10.3%).

However, gains were partially offset by a 4.2% contraction in mining, compared to a 9.8% increase previously, amid declines in both metallic (-4.6%) , including a 6% decrease in copper production, and non-metallic minerals (-1.7%).

Utilities also slipped 0.2% as electricity output dropped 1.2%.

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial output fell 3.6%, reversing a 0.7% gain in May.

data.title: Ametek raises annual profit forecast on steady demand in electromechanical unit
data.description:
Industrial tools maker Ametek raised its annual profit forecast on Thursday, after beating second-quarter estimates, benefiting from steady demand in its electromechanical segment (EMG) and its recent acquisition of FARO Technologies.

Shares of Ametek, which makes testing and measurement instruments, medical devices as well as automation solutions, rose about 6% in early trading following the results.

Quarterly sales at the electromechanical unit, which produces specialized motors, sensors and motion control systems used across industrial and aerospace markets, rose 6% to $618.5 million from a year ago.

The segment has been a key growth driver for the Berwyn, Pennsylvania-based company as manufacturers ramp up automation and efficiency investments despite a mixed macroeconomic backdrop.

"EMG had an excellent quarter highlighted by strong organic sales and orders growth, record-level operating income and robust margin expansion," CEO David Zapico said.

Ametek's purchase of FARO Technologies, which closed earlier this month, also helped the firm expand its market.

The company forecast 2025 adjusted profit in the range of $7.06 to $7.20 per share, above its prior view of $7.02 to $7.18.

It expects annual overall sales to be up mid-single digits, compared with its previous forecast of low-single digit percentage growth.

Ametek earned second-quarter profit of $1.78 per share on an adjusted basis, beating analysts' average estimate of $1.69 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company's quarterly net sales rose to $1.78 billion from $1.74 billion a year ago. Analysts were expecting revenue of $1.73 billion.

data.title: Scripps taps veteran media strategist to bolster research and consumer insights
data.description:
CINCINNATI, July 31, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — The E.W. Scripps Company is enhancing its consumer insights strategy with the appointment of media industry executive Sandy Padula to the post of vice president, head of enterprise research and consumer insights, effective immediately.

In this new role, Padula will spearhead comprehensive enterprise-wide research initiatives that leverage audience and market intelligence across Scripps' multiplatform portfolio of national networks and over 60 local television stations. She also will work with Scripps' sales teams to develop advanced advertising tools that empower brands to optimize campaign effectiveness and accurately measure their impact in real-time.

Padula brings an accomplished record of leadership in media research, with decades of experience working with studios, networks, nonprofits and national brands to translate multiplatform measurement and custom market research into actionable business strategies. Her career includes a tenure at WarnerMedia Entertainment, where she served as senior vice president of research and consumer insight. During her time at WarnerMedia, Padula was instrumental in guiding the company through industry evolution, delivering strategic insights that shaped major content, marketing and advertising initiatives. Most recently, she led a boutique executive consulting firm focused on research-driven growth strategies.

"Sandy's strong expertise in data storytelling and market trends analysis is a tremendous asset to Scripps as we continue deepening our connection to our consumers and advertisers," said Keisha Taylor Starr, Scripps' chief marketing officer and general manager, Scripps Networks. "Her leadership will propel our commitment to innovative, data-informed strategies that drive transformative results for brands and consumers alike."

Padula frequently lectures on the power of storytelling with data at several universities, including the University of Southern California, The University of Alabama and Skidmore College. She will be based in Atlanta.

Media contact:

Becca McCarter, The E.W. Scripps Company, (513) 410-2425, rebecca.mccarter@scripps.com

About Scripps

The E.W. Scripps Company is a diversified media company focused on creating connection. As one of the nation's largest local TV broadcasters, Scripps serves communities with quality, objective local journalism and operates a portfolio of more than 60 stations in 40+ markets. Scripps reaches households across the U.S. with national news outlets Scripps News and Court TV and popular entertainment brands ION, Bounce, Grit, ION Mystery, ION Plus and Laff. Scripps is the nation's largest holder of broadcast spectrum. Scripps Sports serves professional and college sports leagues, conferences and teams with local market depth and national broadcast reach of up to 100% of TV households. Founded in 1878, Scripps is the steward of the Scripps National Spelling Bee, and its longtime motto is: "Give light and the people will find their own way."

Scripps in the news Scripps press releases

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/scripps-taps-veteran-media-strategist-to-bolster-research-and-consumer-insights-302518713.html

SOURCE The E.W. Scripps Company

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Announces an Investigation Against Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical Inc. (RARE) and Encourages Shareholders to Learn More About the Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 31, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical Inc. ("Ultragenyx" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Ultragenyx securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/RARE.

Investigation Details

On July 9, 2025, Ultragenyx and its development partner Mereo BioPharma Group plc issued a press release "announc[ing] that the randomized, placebo-controlled Phase 3 portion of the Orbit study evaluating UX143 (setrusumab) in pediatric and young adult patients with osteogenesis imperfecta (OI) is progressing toward a final analysis[.]" Following a Data Monitoring Committee meeting, the two companies advised that the final analysis would occur "around the end of the year." Following this news, Ultragenyx's stock price fell $10.41 per share, or 25.11%, to close at $31.04 per share on July 10, 2025.

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Announces an Investigation Against Encompass Health Corporation (EHC) and Encourages Shareholders to Learn More About the Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 31, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Encompass Health Corporation ("Encompass" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Encompass securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/EHC.

Investigation Details

On July 15, 2025, The New York Times published an article alleging, among other things, that federal data and inspection reports show that for-profit hospitals run by Encompass perform below average on key safety measures. The article revealed "Encompass owns many of the rehabs with worse rates of potentially preventable, unplanned readmissions to general hospitals" including 34 facilities which "Medicare rated as having statistically significantly worse rates of potentially preventable readmissions." The report further revealed a number of "alarming mistakes" leading to fatalities of patients in their care. Following this news, Encompass stock dropped.

data.title: NetSpark Acquires Infinite Technology Source to Strengthen Managed IT and Telecom Services
data.description:
Expands NetSpark's IT and Telecom Offerings With Deeper Cloud and Cybersecurity Capabilities

DALLAS, TX / ACCESS Newswire / July 31, 2025 / NetSpark IP & Telecom is pleased to announce a strategic investment in Infinite Technology Source, a Louisiana-based provider of managed IT services, cloud solutions, and cybersecurity offerings. This marks NetSpark's seventh investment and reflects the continued execution of the company's growth strategy as it expands its suite of services in managed IT, cloud, and cybersecurity.

Founded in 2001, Infinite Technology Source has earned a trusted reputation for delivering responsive, relationship-driven support in critical service areas such as Microsoft 365 licensing and management, cloud infrastructure, cybersecurity, and VoIP. The company serves clients across healthcare, finance, and professional services, markets where secure, scalable, and reliable IT performance is essential.

"This partnership brings together two organizations that share a commitment to customer success and innovation," said Micah Cooksey, CEO of NetSpark. "Infinite expands our ability to meet customer needs at every stage of the technology lifecycle, from sourcing and implementation to ongoing support. We're excited to offer their services to our broader customer base and to introduce NetSpark's telecom and expense management solutions to theirs."

Darren Autin, Founder of Infinite Technology Source, shared his enthusiasm for the next chapter:

"We're incredibly proud of what we've built at Infinite and excited to bring even more value to our customers as part of the NetSpark platform. What stood out to me most was Micah's vision and leadership. He understands where the industry is going and is building something truly unique. I'm excited to work with him and contribute to a platform that combines world-class services with a deep focus on customer relationships."

Infinite Technology Source will now operate as part of NetSpark's growing Managed Services platform, delivering bundled offerings that integrate telecom sourcing, unified communications, cloud services, and technology lifecycle management. The combined capabilities allow both new and existing customers to streamline operations, simplify vendor relationships, and gain greater visibility and control over their IT and telecom environments.

About NetSpark IP & Telecom

NetSpark IP & Telecom is a platform company focused on investing in and integrating leading businesses in the telecom, connectivity, and managed services sectors. Through a growing network of partner companies, NetSpark delivers tailored solutions in business networks, mobility, cloud infrastructure, cybersecurity, telecom lifecycle management, and expense optimization.

If you are an owner or operator interested in exploring what it looks like to partner with the NetSpark platform, please contact us at info@netsparktelecom.com.

Contact Information

Chris Carn

Chief of Staff

chris.carn@netsparktelecom.com

SOURCE: NetSpark IP & Telecom

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: GreenMerc Subsidiary Subject to Unauthorized Access to Internal Systems
data.description:
GreenMerc AB (publ)

Stockholm, 31 July 2025 | GreenMerc AB (publ) discovered on the morning of July 31 that unauthorized access had occurred to parts of the company's internal systems. An internal investigation has confirmed that unauthorized withdrawals were carried out.

The suspected incident was identified early in the morning. Intruders gained access to internal systems and managed to execute a small number of unauthorized withdrawals from the cryptocurrency exchange Trijo’s so-called hot wallet, where less than 1% of the exchange’s assets are held. Out of approximately 65,000 customers, 27 accounts were affected. Customers impacted by unauthorized withdrawals will be fully compensated by GreenMerc. The total amount withdrawn corresponds to approximately SEK 7.8 million.

Throughout the day, GreenMerc has:

  • Identified how the breach occurred

  • Determined exactly what information was exposed and the financial damage caused

  • Taken measures to secure the systems and prevent future intrusions

In parallel, we are working to ensure that all affected customers are contacted directly and receive clear information about how they may have been impacted.

As there is reason to suspect that personal data from customers of both Trijo and Northcrypto may have been exposed, we have initiated the process of reporting the incident to the relevant authorities. Customers whose personal data may have been compromised will be contacted separately.

GreenMerc prioritizes security and customer trust, and takes this incident with the utmost seriousness.

Affected customers and shareholders are welcome to contact GreenMerc CEO Arvid Börje Ramberg at arvid@greenmerc.com with any further questions.

A Q&A with answers to additional questions regarding the incident is available at:

www.greenmerc.com/q&a

Contact

For further information, please contact:

Arvid Börje Ramberg, arvid@greenmerc.com

About GreenMerc: GreenMerc strives to connect traditional finance and decentralized finance (defi), with the goal of driving growth through the launch of scalable web-based financial services. The company focuses on innovative technology solutions and offers a broad portfolio of services, from development to finished, deployed and managed solutions, enabling a more accessible and transparent financial market for GreenMerc’s customers. GreenMerc is listed on NGM Nordic SME with the ticker GMERC B. Read more at www.greenmerc.com.

About Trijo: Trijo is a subsidiary of GreenMerc and Sweden’s leading crypto exchange. Trijo is a pioneer in the Swedish cryptocurrency market and specializes in digital currency services. With an app available for both iPhone and Android, and support for 260+ new currencies, Trijo works to provide easier access to cryptocurrencies. Trijo has also launched a service that enables staking, which yields returns on digital assets. Read more at www.trijo.se.

https://news.cision.com/greenmerc-ab--publ-/r/greenmerc-subsidiary-subject-to-unauthorized-access-to-internal-systems%2Cc4213247

data.title: HONG YUAN HOLDING GROUP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Hong Yuan Holding Group, a company specializing in supply chain management services with a focus on the wholesale and internet sales of fast-moving consumer goods in China, has released its latest Form 10-Q report. The report highlights a significant financial turnaround and provides insights into the company's operational performance and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $241.065 million, reflecting the consolidation of the Chinese VIEs under common control which started generating revenue in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $116.718 million, indicating a strong margin from the newly consolidated operations.
  • Net Income from operations: $40.138 million, a significant improvement from the previous year's loss, driven by increased revenue and controlled operating expenses.
  • Net Income: $37.357 million, compared to a net loss of $46.554 million in the same period last year, highlighting a turnaround in profitability.
  • Basic and dilutive net income (loss) per common share: $0.00, reflecting the improved financial performance while maintaining the same number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue of $241.065 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, primarily due to the consolidation of Chinese VIEs under common control, which began generating revenue in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are primarily focused in China, with the consolidation of Chinese VIEs contributing significantly to the revenue increase. This indicates a strategic focus on the Chinese market for supply chain management services.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on supply chain management services, particularly in the wholesale and internet sales of fast-moving consumer goods in China. Management is also seeking additional equity or debt financing to sustain operations and achieve profitability.

SEC Filing: HONG YUAN HOLDING GROUP [ HGYN ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: Canada’s Wage Growth Slows in May
data.description:
Average weekly earnings in Canada rose by 3.3% year-over-year to CAD 1,294 in May 2025, easing from a downwardly revised 4.3% gain in April.

The deceleration in wage growth was broad-based across sectors and reflects a mix of wage adjustments, changes in employment composition, hours worked, and base effects.

The most notable slowdowns were seen in oil and gas extraction (3.1% vs 7.2%) and support activities for mining and energy (2.8% vs 7.1%).

In contrast, earnings in mining and quarrying accelerated (3.4% vs 1.5%).

On a monthly basis, average weekly earnings were little changed.

Meanwhile, average weekly hours worked declined slightly to 33.3 hours in May, down from 33.5 hours in both April 2025 and May 2024.

data.title: CoreWeave shares jump on AI demand; Citigroup upgrades rating
data.description:
** AI cloud computing firm CoreWeave shares jump 10.5% to $113.7 in early trading

** Citigroup upgrades rating to "buy" from "neutral"; says they have a stronger picture on AI demand highlighted by Microsoft results

** Brokerage adds that existing multi-year contracts with MSFT, OpenAI and others will drive revenue growth

** Wall Street's AI heavyweights fuel $500 bln gain in stock market value with results from MSFT and Meta Platforms showing investments in tech are paying off

** Morgan Stanley raises PT on CRWV to $91 from $58; says given the strong demand backdrop, business momentum and impressive Q1 upside, they expect a strong beat

** Co closes $2.6 bln secured debt financing, strengthening its market position as AI cloud leader

** Average rating of 24 analysts is "hold"; median PT is $80
